hypothesis: Combining EMA breakout with OBV confirmation and volume oscillator peak filters out weak trends and reduces whipsaw in low-liquidity periods, while ATR-based risk management ensures consistent risk-reward profiles across varying volatility regimes.

Combining EMA breakout with OBV confirmation and volume oscillator filtering creates a robust trend continuation signal that avoids false breakouts in low-liquidity conditions, while ATR-based risk management ensures consistent risk-reward profiles across varying volatility regimes

Combining EMA trend filter with OBV momentum confirmation and Volume Oscillator peak detection creates a robust breakout signal that avoids false signals during low-liquidity periods while ensuring sufficient momentum for profitable moves. The ATR-based risk management ensures position sizing adapts to volatility, preventing overexposure during high-volatility regimes.
